\section{Adaptive Reaching Paradigm}
\label{sec:adaptive_reaching_paradigm}

Figure~\ref{fig:schematic} summarizes the task structure used throughout the study.
An agent must generate goal-directed reaches in a two-dimensional workspace while avoiding a central obstacle and compensating for externally imposed lateral perturbations.
Control is implemented by a PPO policy operating in closed loop with the point-mass environment, receiving state observations and reward feedback at each timestep.

\begin{figure*}[t]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.96\textwidth]{figures/figure1_schematic.pdf}
\caption{
Schematic of the adaptive goal-directed reaching task and control architecture.
On the left, the agent starts from a fixed initial position and must reach a goal while avoiding an obstacle and compensating for transient lateral perturbations.
On the right, a PPO controller interacts with the point-mass environment in closed loop through observation, action, and reward signals.
}
\label{fig:schematic}
\end{figure*}

\section{Neural Population Results}
\label{sec:neural_results}

\begin{figure*}[t]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.98\textwidth]{../experiments/version_2_0/results/neural_analysis/manuscript/figure1_neural_summary.pdf}
\caption{
Neural population summary across perturbation conditions.
The panel combines low-dimensional latent structure (policy and value PCA), representational similarity analysis (RSA), and condition decoding confusion matrices.
Together, these analyses quantify separation and overlap of neural representations across $P0$, $L1$--$L3$, and $R1$--$R3$.
}
\label{fig:neural_summary}
\end{figure*}

\begin{figure*}[t]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.98\textwidth]{../experiments/version_2_0/results/neural_analysis/manuscript/figure2_neural_pca_3d.pdf}
\caption{
Three-dimensional PCA of latent neural activity.
Policy and value latent trajectories are shown in the first three principal components to visualize the geometry of perturbation-dependent neural manifolds.
}
\label{fig:neural_pca_3d}
\end{figure*}

\begin{figure*}[t]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.98\textwidth]{../experiments/version_2_0/results/neural_analysis/manuscript/figure3_neural_trajectories.pdf}
\caption{
Neural state trajectories through latent PCA space.
Each curve traces the temporal evolution of a single episode, with line style distinguishing successful and failed trials.
These trajectories reveal condition-specific dynamics over time.
}
\label{fig:neural_trajectories}
\end{figure*}

\begin{figure*}[t]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.95\textwidth]{../experiments/version_2_0/results/neural_analysis/manuscript/figure4_success_failure.pdf}
\caption{
Success-versus-failure comparison of latent neural states.
Distributions of principal component scores indicate how neural population activity differs between successful and unsuccessful episodes.
}
\label{fig:success_failure}
\end{figure*}

\section{Behavioural Statistical Results}
\label{sec:behavioural_statistics}

The full behavioural statistical summary for Experiment 2 is reported in Tables~\ref{tab:descriptive_statistics}--\ref{tab:pairwise_posthoc}.
Descriptive statistics are provided first, followed by assumption checks, omnibus tests across perturbation conditions, and corrected nonparametric pairwise post hoc comparisons.
Because the majority of variables violated normality or homogeneity assumptions, the primary inferential results are reported using Kruskal-Wallis tests with Holm-corrected Mann-Whitney follow-up comparisons.

\input{tables/table1_descriptive_statistics}
